topic.what: Impact of post-quantum certificate chain sizes on TLS time to first byte (TTFB) latency
topic.how: Empirical simulations using OQS library with artificially inflated certificate chains plus real-world NCSA Zeek-monitored TLS traffic analysis
topic.why_matters: PQC certificates increase sizes 5x-20x causing discrete latency jumps when exceeding transport layer flight limits, critically affecting CDN infrastructure handling millions of connections

characteristics.what_it_measures: End-to-end latency from TCP connection through TLS handshake to first HTTP response byte
characteristics.evidence: Tables VI and VII across 0-200ms RTT values
characteristics.context: ML-DSA and ECDSA stay within flight limits while SLH-DSA incurs +1 RTT penalty

characteristics.what_it_measures: Relative size increase of post-quantum certificates vs classical
characteristics.evidence: Abstract and Table I showing ML-DSA (8-15KB) and SLH-DSA (60-150KB) chains
characteristics.context: Depends on specific PQC algorithm used

characteristics.what_itasures: Percentage of TLS connections using session resumption on CDN endpoints
characteristics.evidence: Table III from NCSA Zeek-monitored traffic
characteristics.context: 16-month dataset from NCSA national HPC system

characteristics.what_it_measures: Percentage of connections using TLS 1.3 protocol
characteristics.evidence: Table III showing CDN leads in TLS 1.3 adoption
characteristics.context: Correlated with session resumption rates over 16 months

characteristics.what_it_measures: Certificate chain size where additional RTT is incurred due to bandwidth window limits
characteristics.evidence: Figure 5 showing discrete TTFB jumps at 10KB and 40KB boundaries
characteristics.context: TCP initial congestion window (IW) limits ~14KB for first RTT transmission

characteristics.what_it_measures: Multiplier of certificate chain size supportable while staying under flight limits
characteristics.evidence: Section V-C and Table VIII showing MTC enables 10-18KB (1 int.) and 10-27KB (2 int.) ranges
characteristics.context: MTC replaces intermediate certificates with Merkle proofs (~700-900 bytes)

characteristics.what_itasures: Multiplier of certificate chain size supportable with CDN chain optimizations
characteristics.evidence: Table VIII showing CDN optimizations allow 10-17KB chains vs baseline
characteristics.context: Based on 25-40% chain size reduction approximations from prior studies

characteristics.what_it_measures: Time to first byte savings from session resumption for CDNs vs non-CDNs
characteristics.evidence: Figure 7 showing ~2x more TTFB savings for CDNs due to higher resumption rates
characteristics.context: Savings scale with certificate chain size crossing flight thresholds

not_for: Extremely low-RTT local networks where OQS overhead dominates
requires: OQS library or similar PQC support

novelty: Isolates certificate chain size as primary independent variable affecting TTFB
Identifies discrete transport layer flight limits (10KB, 40KB) causing TTFB jumps
First measurement of certificate chain size impact on TTFB with NCSA real-world data
Compares MTC and CDN optimizations as chain size reduction techniques
Quantifies session resumption savings correlation with TLS 1.3 adoption over 16 months
open_problems: Testing fragmentation and packet loss effects for varying certificate chain sizes
Implementing and testing real MTC configurations in production environments
Developing more precise ASN-based CDN classification methods
Examining how CDN properties affect latency under real-world conditions
Running certificate chain simulations on actual CDN infrastructure
